tool_rules.title: Command Execution Tool
tool_rules.impact: Allows arbitrary command execution on the host system, leading to full server compromise
tool_rules.remediation: Restrict to allowlisted commands only, run in sandboxed environment, require strong authentication and audit logging

tool_rules.title: Arbitrary File Read
tool_rules.impact: Allows reading arbitrary files including secrets, credentials, private keys, and configuration files
tool_rules.remediation: Restrict to a specific directory with a chroot/allowlist, validate and sanitize all path inputs

tool_rules.title: Arbitrary File Write
tool_rules.impact: Allows writing arbitrary files; attacker can plant backdoors, overwrite config files, or inject malicious content
tool_rules.remediation: Restrict write paths to specific safe directories, validate content, require strong authentication

tool_rules.title: File Deletion Tool
tool_rules.impact: Allows deleting arbitrary files, enabling denial of service or destruction of critical data
tool_rules.remediation: Restrict deletion to specific safe directories, implement soft-delete with audit logs

tool_rules.title: HTTP/Network Request Tool (SSRF Risk)
tool_rules.impact: Allows server-side requests to arbitrary URLs, enabling SSRF to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, and private networks
tool_rules.remediation: Implement URL allowlisting, block private IP ranges and metadata endpoints, use egress filtering

tool_rules.title: Database Query Tool (Injection Risk)
tool_rules.impact: Allows arbitrary database queries, enabling data exfiltration, schema enumeration, and potential database privilege escalation
tool_rules.remediation: Use parameterized queries only, restrict to read-only operations where possible, implement row-level security

tool_rules.title: Directory Listing / File Enumeration
tool_rules.impact: Enables enumeration of filesystem structure, discovery of sensitive files and directories
tool_rules.remediation: Restrict to specific safe directories, disable recursive listing, require authentication

tool_rules.title: Environment Variable Access
tool_rules.impact: Leaks environment variables which commonly contain API keys, passwords, database credentials, and tokens
tool_rules.remediation: Never expose environment variables via tools, use secrets managers instead

tool_rules.title: Code Evaluation / Scripting
tool_rules.impact: Allows execution of arbitrary code in the server's runtime environment
tool_rules.remediation: Remove code evaluation capabilities entirely or sandbox completely with no network/filesystem access

tool_rules.title: Admin / Privileged Operations
tool_rules.impact: Provides direct access to privileged operations or allows privilege escalation
tool_rules.remediation: Remove admin tools from MCP exposure, require multi-factor authentication and explicit approval workflows

tool_rules.title: Process / System Management
tool_rules.impact: Allows process manipulation which can be used to kill security tools, launch backdoors, or crash services
tool_rules.remediation: Remove process management from MCP interface, implement strict allowlisting if required

tool_rules.title: Secret / Credential Management Access
tool_rules.impact: Direct access to credential stores; compromise allows lateral movement to all connected systems
tool_rules.remediation: Never expose secret retrieval through MCP, use service accounts with minimal permissions

tool_rules.title: Network / Socket Operations
tool_rules.impact: Enables internal network scanning, lateral movement, and connection to internal services
tool_rules.remediation: Block arbitrary network connections, use allowlisted service endpoints only

tool_rules.title: Cloud Metadata / Configuration Access
tool_rules.impact: Access to cloud instance metadata including IAM credentials, allowing cloud environment takeover
tool_rules.remediation: Block access to metadata endpoints, disable IMDSv1, require IMDSv2 with hop limit 1

resource_rules.title: Sensitive System File Exposed
resource_rules.impact: Direct exposure of system credentials and key material
resource_rules.remediation: Remove all system file resources immediately

resource_rules.title: Secret / Credential Resource Exposed
resource_rules.impact: Leaks secrets, API keys, and credentials enabling access to downstream systems
resource_rules.remediation: Remove credential resources from MCP, use secrets management services

resource_rules.title: Internal Network Resource Exposed
resource_rules.impact: Exposes internal network services, enabling lateral movement and internal reconnaissance
resource_rules.remediation: Never expose internal network resources, use proper service meshes with authentication

resource_rules.title: Cloud Metadata Endpoint Exposed
resource_rules.impact: Cloud metadata endpoint exposure leaks IAM credentials, enabling cloud account takeover
resource_rules.remediation: Block metadata endpoint access at network level, remove from resources immediately

resource_rules.title: Log / Debug Information Exposed
resource_rules.impact: Log files may contain sensitive data, authentication tokens, internal IPs, and application secrets
resource_rules.remediation: Remove log resources from MCP, implement proper log management with access controls

resource_rules.title: Configuration File Exposed
resource_rules.impact: Configuration files frequently contain database credentials, API keys, and internal service addresses
resource_rules.remediation: Restrict configuration file access, use environment variables for secrets

prompt_rules.title: Prompt Injection Attack Surface
prompt_rules.impact: Prompts accepting user input without sanitization are vulnerable to injection attacks that override agent instructions
prompt_rules.remediation: Implement input sanitization, use structured outputs, add injection detection

prompt_rules.title: System Instruction Override Risk
prompt_rules.impact: System-level prompts with mutable parameters allow attackers to override agent behavior
prompt_rules.remediation: Never allow user control over system-level instructions

prompt_rules.title: Data Leakage via Prompt Template
prompt_rules.impact: Prompts that inject large data contexts may allow exfiltration via the model response
prompt_rules.remediation: Limit context injection, implement output filtering, monitor for exfiltration patterns
